title: Biogen's controversial Alzheimer's drug approval, the billionaire space race, real estate, Bitcoin & more
description:
Follow the besties:

https://twitter.com/chamath

https://linktr.ee/calacanis

https://twitter.com/DavidSacks

https://twitter.com/friedberg

Follow the pod:

https://twitter.com/theallinpod

https://linktr.ee/allinpodcast

Show Notes:

0:00 Bestie intro, Chamath's whereabouts

2:54 Breaking down Biogen's Alzheimer's drug & the FDA's controversial approval

26:46 Billionaire space race: Is Bezos going to space too risky or a smart marketing move? How the space industry is a perfect example of effective government intervention. Can anyone dominate space?

42:55 Big Tech getting back to the office: what is the new normal for companies with thousands of employees?

56:49 Recent factors on the commercial & private real estate markets, inflation head fake

1:05:01 ProPublica's report on billionaire tax returns

1:15:58 Bitcoin toxicity, Snowflake's CEO apologizes for comments on merit & diversity

1:32:48 Science corner with Friedberg: Induced pluripotent stem cells & cloning

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses various topics including Biogen's Alzheimer's drug approval, the billionaire space race, real estate, and Bitcoin. The hosts also touch on Big Tech returning to the office and ProPublica's report on billionaire tax returns. The episode features a science corner with Friedberg on induced pluripotent stem cells and cloning.

takeaways.text: Biogen's Alzheimer's drug approval is controversial
Billionaire space race is a marketing move
Big Tech is returning to the office
Real estate market is affected by inflation
ProPublica's report reveals billionaire tax avoidance
Induced pluripotent stem cells have potential

sentiment.notes: The hosts have varying opinions on the topics discussed, with some being optimistic and others being cautious.

notable_moments.description: Discussion on Biogen's Alzheimer's drug approval
Big Tech returning to the office
